what_it_does: Renders a fixed-size visual calibration board for a zone colour palette, showing surface swatches, a page-to-plate-to-nested depth example, and an accent colour axis using CSS custom properties.
observations: The document is a standalone HTML page with inline CSS and a single 1600 by 900 artboard.
It loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts and imports /surface/styles/vars.css for design tokens.
The layout uses absolute positioning for the title, section labels, decorative plan grid, footer hatch, and circular mark.
Surface chips display Page, Warm plate, Cool plate, Nested box, and Gold wash using CSS variables rather than literal colour values.
The lower area compares a nested depth stack with an accent-axis swatch strip and two small mix/body samples.
Visible text states that values were sampled from source slides and emphasizes subtle warm-to-cool surface separation at similar lightness.
standouts: The file is explicitly framed as a palette calibration artifact rather than an application screen.
It pairs abstract swatches with a composed depth example to show how the colours behave in use.
The labels expose token names such as var(--page), var(--warm), var(--gold), and var(--body).
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties for colour, spacing, radius, and type scale.
Uses a reset at the top of the style block and file-local component class names.
Combines external token CSS with inline element styles for per-chip colours and label positions.
Uses monospace text for token labels and small uppercase labels for section headings.
Breaks responsive web conventions by targeting a fixed 1600 by 900 canvas.
concerns: The page depends on /surface/styles/vars.css for all core colour values, so the displayed palette cannot be interpreted from this file alone.
Several elements use inline styles for positioning and colour assignment, which makes the structure less reusable.
The .hex labels show variable names rather than actual hex values, despite the class name.
The fixed artboard and absolute placement may not adapt to smaller or larger viewports.
notes: This file is a visual token calibration sheet for inspecting subtle surface colours, depth, and accent relationships.
questions: What are the actual resolved values of the CSS variables from /surface/styles/vars.css?
What source slides were sampled to produce the palette?
Is the 1600 by 900 canvas an export target or an interactive viewport assumption?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the rendered content: it presents a sampled zone-colour calibration, although the file itself shows token references rather than raw sampled colour values.
intention_signals: It seems intended to document and demonstrate how a subtle palette should separate page, plate, nested, wash, and accent roles inside a controlled visual specimen.
novelty: The page treats colour tokens as a calibrated specimen board, combining swatches, explanatory copy, and a small hierarchy mockup in one fixed composition.
contribution: Provides a neutral palette-calibration artifact that a fused system would keep as evidence for surface roles, token naming, accent usage, and depth treatment through borders and shadows.
summary_for_embedding: A standalone fixed-canvas HTML calibration board displaying CSS-token surface swatches, a depth stack, and an accent colour axis for a sampled zone palette.
intention_for_embedding: This was for documenting and visually testing subtle near-white surface separation and restrained accent colours from a sampled source palette.
